summary: Phase 4 closeout regression — backend-only. Ran 26 pytest cases against /api covering auth proxy (login/me/refresh/logout/signup with rate-limit handling), inventory-movements list (v5 migration), parts CRUD with supplier_id round-trip (v6 migration), wipe-all + sync zero-counts, journal side-effects for invoice/expense/purchase (Phase 4.6 double-entry), and smoke CRUD for suppliers/customers/expenses/purchases/accounts. All 26 tests passed.

minor.issue: Without SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY auto_confirm cannot be honored — Supabase falls back to email-confirm flow and the project's default SMTP returns 429 'over_email_send_rate_limit'. Backend proxy correctly forwards the upstream status; this is an env-configuration limitation, not a code bug. Test accepts 200 or 429 as documented.

action_items: (Optional) Add SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY to /app/backend/.env so /api/auth/signup?auto_confirm=true can issue users that can log in immediately — required for E2E signup→login automation.

(Optional) Wipe-all currently skips the journal_entries and inventory_movements tables — TEST_-prefixed entries accumulate. Consider adding both to WIPE_TABLES or expose a separate /api/admin/wipe-ledger endpoint.
critical_code_review_comments: main.py is 1072 lines and contains every CRUD endpoint + helpers + journal hook + wipe + analytics. Above the 700-line threshold — split into routers/parts.py, routers/invoices.py, routers/inventory.py, routers/admin.py for maintainability.

wipe-all endpoint has no auth dependency (no Depends(bearer_user)). It can permanently wipe all business data with a single unauthenticated POST. This is a major production risk even with 'soft mode' caveat.

Inventory side-effects on POST /api/invoices (stock decrement) and journal upserts are not in a DB transaction — partial failure leaves the system in an inconsistent state. Consider wrapping in a Postgres RPC or compensating-write pattern.

routers/inventory_movements.py and routers/journal.py both do a late `from main import get_supabase` inside _supabase(). Functional but creates a circular import smell — lift get_supabase into a dedicated dependencies module.

/api/auth/logout currently calls bearer_user (validates JWT) but never actually invalidates the refresh token at Supabase — comment says it does but the body just returns {success: true}. Either call POST /auth/v1/logout upstream or update the comment.
/api/parts list with low_stock=true fetches all rows then filters in Python — acknowledged in code comment but will become O(N) hot path; consider adding a Postgres view or RPC.
PartModel.supplier_id is Optional[str] with no FK validation at the API layer — caller could send an arbitrary string. Acceptable for soft-mode, but document or validate.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates TEST_-prefixed rows and best-effort cleans them in teardown_module. Journal entries (JE-INV-*, JE-EXP-*, JE-PUR-*) and inventory_movements rows are NOT cleaned because wipe-all does not include those tables.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_backend.py and finishes in ~20s with 26 passing tests. To re-run: `cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_phase4_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Login token TTL is 1h — re-run from scratch if tests are stale. Auth signup test tolerates 429 (Supabase SMTP rate-limit) until SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY is added. Frontend was not tested in this round.
